_instructions.pdf_support: Place a PDF as 'slides.pdf' in the slides folder for embedded PDF viewer

title: What is a Microcontroller?
subtitle: UNO & ESP — Smart Brain of Electronics
overview: This session introduces students to microcontrollers using real-life examples. Students will learn how Arduino UNO and ESP boards act like smart brains that control LEDs, motors, sensors, and smart devices.

images._note: Add photos of Arduino UNO, ESP8266, students coding, and simple electronic projects.

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Photo of Arduino UNO board
gallery.caption: Arduino UNO — Beginner friendly microcontroller board

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Photo of ESP8266 NodeMCU board
gallery.caption: ESP8266 — WiFi enabled smart microcontroller
Robots use microcontrollers as their brain

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Students uploading code to Arduino
gallery.caption: Students learning coding with Arduino IDE
Traffic lights are controlled using microcontrollers

keyTopics: Microcontroller — Small Smart Brain that controls electronics
Arduino UNO — Beginner friendly programmable board
Programming — Giving instructions to the microcontroller
Real Life Examples — Washing machine, traffic lights, robots
USB Cable — Used to upload code into the board

activity.title: Blink LED using Arduino UNO
steps: Watch LED blink ON and OFF

quiz.explanation: A microcontroller is a tiny smart computer chip that controls electronic devices.
Arduino UNO is very beginner friendly and easy to program.

quiz.q: What special feature does ESP8266 have?
quiz.explanation: ESP8266 can connect to WiFi and create IoT projects.

quiz.q: Which software is used to program Arduino UNO?
quiz.explanation: Arduino IDE is used to write and upload code.

quiz.q: Which device can be controlled using a microcontroller?
quiz.explanation: Microcontrollers can control many electronic devices.

quiz.q: What is the function of USB cable in Arduino?
options: To upload code and give power
quiz.explanation: USB cable provides power and uploads code to Arduino.
Microcontrollers are used in many smart electronic systems.
Microcontrollers work according to the program uploaded into them.
Sensors and buttons send information to the microcontroller.
LED is an output device because it shows results from the microcontroller.

quiz.q: What happens after uploading code to Arduino?
quiz.explanation: After uploading code, Arduino performs the programmed task.

quiz.q: What is the nickname of a microcontroller?
quiz.explanation: Microcontroller is called a smart brain because it controls electronic devices.

quiz.q: Which board is commonly used for IoT projects?
quiz.explanation: ESP8266 is widely used in IoT because of its WiFi feature.

quiz.q: What does UNO mean in Arduino UNO?
Which activity is best for beginners?
quiz.explanation: LED Blink is the first simple project for learning microcontrollers.
